hypothesis: Incorporating DFT+U corrections specifically parameterized for the problematic 3d transition metals (Cr, Mn, Fe, Co, Ni) as an intermediate fidelity level within a multi-fidelity ∆-learning cascade—where the first ∆ corrects GFN1-xTB to DFT+U and the second ∆ corrects DFT+U to HSE06—will yield greater band gap prediction accuracy for transition-metal-containing MOFs than the current two-level GFN1-xTB-to-HSE06 ∆-learning scheme, because the explicit treatment of strong d-electron correlation at the intermediate level will reduce the systematic underestimation errors that currently cluster transition-metal MOFs as outliers in the descriptor space.
